exercises.name: 1 Mile Flat Run (Easy)
exercises.simplyPut: Get your heart pumping with 1 Mile Flat Run (Easy) — a beginner-level cardiovascular exercise that improves endurance, burns calories, and strengthens your legs.

mistakes.error: Too much too soon (sudden volume increase)
mistakes.consequence: Overuse injuries: shin splints, stress fractures, IT band syndrome
mistakes.correction: 10% rule: increase weekly volume max 10%; include recovery weeks

mistakes.error: Ignoring rest days
mistakes.consequence: Overtraining syndrome: fatigue, illness, hormonal disruption, injury
mistakes.correction: Minimum 1-2 rest days per week; listen to body; track HRV

mistakes.error: Wrong shoe for activity
mistakes.consequence: Blisters, plantar fasciitis, shin splints, ankle instability
mistakes.correction: Running shoes for running (replace every 400-500 miles); cycling shoes for cycling

mistakes.error: Skipping cool-down
mistakes.consequence: Blood pooling, delayed DOMS onset, stiffness
mistakes.correction: 5-10 min light movement + stretching post-workout

mistakes.error: Only doing one type of cardio
mistakes.consequence: Overuse of specific movement pattern; imbalanced fitness
mistakes.correction: Cross-train: run + swim + cycle; varies loading patterns

mistakes.error: Comparing to others instead of personal progress
mistakes.consequence: Overexertion, burnout, abandoning exercise entirely
mistakes.correction: Track personal metrics: pace, distance, HR zones; celebrate progress

physiologyLinks.effect: Moderate cardio reduces systolic BP by 5-8 mmHg (ACSM position stand)
Aerobic fitness decreases RHR by 10-20 bpm over months
Brain-derived neurotrophic factor increases 2-3x during cardio (Rasmussen 2009)
Regular aerobic training improves VO2max 15-20% in 12 weeks (ACSM)
Aerobic exercise increases central serotonin, improving mood

mistakes.error: Too much too soon (sudden volume increase)

mistakes.error: Ignoring rest days

mistakes.error: Wrong shoe for activity

mistakes.error: Only doing one type of cardio

exercises.name: 3 Mile Treadmill Run (Moderate)
exercises.simplyPut: Get your heart pumping with 3 Mile Treadmill Run (Moderate) — a intermediate-level cardiovascular exercise that improves endurance, burns calories, and strengthens your legs.

mistakes.error: Too much too soon (sudden volume increase)
mistakes.consequence: Overuse injuries: shin splints, stress fractures, IT band syndrome
mistakes.correction: 10% rule: increase weekly volume max 10%; include recovery weeks

mistakes.error: Ignoring rest days
mistakes.consequence: Overtraining syndrome: fatigue, illness, hormonal disruption, injury
mistakes.correction: Minimum 1-2 rest days per week; listen to body; track HRV

mistakes.error: Wrong shoe for activity
mistakes.consequence: Blisters, plantar fasciitis, shin splints, ankle instability
mistakes.correction: Running shoes for running (replace every 400-500 miles); cycling shoes for cycling

mistakes.error: Skipping cool-down
mistakes.consequence: Blood pooling, delayed DOMS onset, stiffness
mistakes.correction: 5-10 min light movement + stretching post-workout

mistakes.error: Only doing one type of cardio
mistakes.consequence: Overuse of specific movement pattern; imbalanced fitness
mistakes.correction: Cross-train: run + swim + cycle; varies loading patterns

mistakes.error: Comparing to others instead of personal progress
mistakes.consequence: Overexertion, burnout, abandoning exercise entirely
mistakes.correction: Track personal metrics: pace, distance, HR zones; celebrate progress

exercises.name: Basic Jump Rope
exercises.simplyPut: Swing a rope over your head and jump over it as it passes under your feet. Jump rope is the boxer's secret weapon — it builds coordination, footwork, cardiovascular endurance, and calf strength in one simple tool that fits in your pocket.
exercises.howTo:
1. Hold handles at hip height, elbows close to body
2. Use wrists to turn the rope (not shoulders)
3. Jump just high enough to clear the rope (1-2 inches)
4. Land on balls of feet, never on heels
5. Keep knees slightly bent
6. Look straight ahead, not at the rope
7. Start with 30-second intervals, rest 30 seconds
8. Progress to continuous jumping

exercises.name: Burpees
exercises.simplyPut: Drop to the floor, do a push-up, jump your feet forward, then leap into the air. It's the exercise everyone loves to hate because it works everything — heart, lungs, legs, arms, and core — all in one explosive movement.
exercises.howTo:
1. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart
2. Squat down and place hands on the floor
3. Jump or step feet back to plank position
4. Perform a push-up (chest to floor)
5. Jump or step feet forward to squat position
6. Explosively jump up with arms overhead
7. Land softly with knees slightly bent
8. Immediately begin next rep

exercises.name: Easy Pace Running
exercises.simplyPut: Run at a comfortable pace where you can hold a conversation. This is the foundation of all running fitness — it builds your aerobic base, teaches your body to burn fat for fuel, and makes your heart and lungs more efficient. Most elite runners do 80% of their training at easy pace.
exercises.howTo:
1. Start walking briskly for 2-3 minutes to warm up
2. Gradually transition to a light jog
3. Find a pace where you can speak in full sentences (the talk test)
4. If you're gasping, you're too fast — slow down
5. Land with midfoot under your hips, not ahead of you
6. Keep cadence around 170-180 steps per minute
7. Arms relaxed at 90°, hands loose (imagine holding potato chips)
8. Breathe naturally — in through nose and mouth
9. Duration matters more than speed at easy pace

exercises.name: Freestyle Swimming Laps
exercises.simplyPut: Swim back and forth across the pool using the freestyle (front crawl) stroke. Swimming is the ultimate full-body, zero-impact exercise — it works every major muscle while being gentle on joints. It's therapy and training in one.
exercises.howTo:
1. Push off wall in streamline position (arms extended, body tight)
2. Begin flutter kick — small, fast kicks from the hips
3. Rotate body side to side with each arm stroke
4. Reach forward, catch the water, and pull it past your hip
5. Breathe by turning head to the side (not lifting) every 2-3 strokes
6. Exhale underwater through nose, inhale quickly when head turns
7. Keep head neutral — look at pool bottom, not forward
8. Flip turn at walls for continuous swimming

exercises.name: Half Marathon Trail Run (Easy)
exercises.simplyPut: Get your heart pumping with Half Marathon Trail Run (Easy) — a beginner-level cardiovascular exercise that improves endurance, burns calories, and strengthens your legs.

mistakes.error: Too much too soon (sudden volume increase)
mistakes.consequence: Overuse injuries: shin splints, stress fractures, IT band syndrome
mistakes.correction: 10% rule: increase weekly volume max 10%; include recovery weeks

mistakes.error: Ignoring rest days
mistakes.consequence: Overtraining syndrome: fatigue, illness, hormonal disruption, injury
mistakes.correction: Minimum 1-2 rest days per week; listen to body; track HRV

mistakes.error: Wrong shoe for activity
mistakes.consequence: Blisters, plantar fasciitis, shin splints, ankle instability
mistakes.correction: Running shoes for running (replace every 400-500 miles); cycling shoes for cycling

mistakes.error: Skipping cool-down
mistakes.consequence: Blood pooling, delayed DOMS onset, stiffness
mistakes.correction: 5-10 min light movement + stretching post-workout

mistakes.error: Only doing one type of cardio
mistakes.consequence: Overuse of specific movement pattern; imbalanced fitness
mistakes.correction: Cross-train: run + swim + cycle; varies loading patterns

mistakes.error: Comparing to others instead of personal progress
mistakes.consequence: Overexertion, burnout, abandoning exercise entirely
mistakes.correction: Track personal metrics: pace, distance, HR zones; celebrate progress

mistakes.correction: Running shoes for running (replace every 400-500 miles); cycling shoes for cycling

mistakes.error: Skipping cool-down
mistakes.consequence: Blood pooling, delayed DOMS onset, stiffness
mistakes.correction: 5-10 min light movement + stretching post-workout

mistakes.error: Only doing one type of cardio
mistakes.consequence: Overuse of specific movement pattern; imbalanced fitness
mistakes.correction: Cross-train: run + swim + cycle; varies loading patterns

mistakes.error: Comparing to others instead of personal progress
mistakes.consequence: Overexertion, burnout, abandoning exercise entirely
mistakes.correction: Track personal metrics: pace, distance, HR zones; celebrate progress

physiologyLinks.effect: Aerobic fitness decreases RHR by 10-20 bpm over months
Aerobic exercise increases HDL by 5-10% (meta-analysis: Kodama 2007)
Moderate cardio <60 min has minimal cortisol impact; >90 min significantly elevates
Regular aerobic training improves VO2max 15-20% in 12 weeks (ACSM)
